topics.title: Overhead top-down view toggle added to snake game board
topics.trigger: The user wanted a way to view the snake game board from directly above, flattening the isometric 3D perspective into a simple top-down layout. After an initial attempt, the snake and food blocks still appeared as 3D cubes rather than flat squares, so a follow-up fix was needed.
topics.response:
- Added `toggleOverheadView()` in `src/main.ts`, bound to the `O` key. When activated, it saves the current isometric angle, perspective ratio, and perspective strength, then zeroes them all out to produce a flat axis-aligned view. Pressing `O` again restores the saved values exactly.
- Fixed block rendering in overhead mode by making `baseBlockHeight` conditional: when overhead view is active the extruded cube height is set to zero, so all snake segments and food render as flat squares rather than head-on cubes.
- Rotation (`Q`/`E`) and perspective (`[`/`]`) key handlers now return early when overhead view is active, and the auto-rotate loop skips its angle update while overhead is on. Controls text in all three game modes (single, pvp, bvb) was updated to mention the new key.
topics.decisions:
- **Two-pass fix for true flatness**: The first implementation only zeroed the camera angles and perspective, which was not enough — the blocks themselves still had extruded height and appeared as cubes viewed straight-on. Rather than adding a separate rendering path, the block height calculation was made aware of the overhead flag, keeping the fix minimal and contained to a single expression.
- **Save-and-restore over a fixed overhead preset**: When entering overhead view the current isometric settings are saved to private fields and restored on exit, rather than snapping back to hardcoded defaults. This means users who have customised the rotation angle or perspective before pressing `O` get their exact view back, with no loss of state.
- **Input lockout during overhead view**: Rotation and perspective keys are silently ignored while overhead is active, and auto-rotation is suspended. The alternative — allowing those keys to work and immediately re-flattening — would have been confusing. Locking input keeps the overhead view stable and predictable without requiring a separate mode indicator in the UI.

recap: The snake game gained an overhead view mode, accessible by pressing the `O` key during any game mode. Activating it collapses the isometric 3D board into a true top-down layout where the board, snake segments, and food all appear as flat squares with no depth or perspective. The view locks out rotation and perspective controls for the duration, and auto-rotation pauses automatically. Pressing `O` a second time returns the board to exactly the isometric angle and perspective settings the player had before switching, preserving any customisation they had made.
